entry.title: Accelerating SAP ICSM and IRT integration with pre-packaged sample content
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management (ICSM) with IRT/RTSM systems is essential for running efficient clinical trials but many teams still end up reinventing the same integration patterns.

To help the ecosystem move faster, sample integration content for ICSM is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, providing a practical starting point for building integrations between ICSM and IRT systems using SAP Integration Suite.

This post introduces the content, highlights the supported ICSM-IRT scenarios and explains how partners and customers can reuse and extend these building blocks to accelerate real‑world integrations.

Business value

Instead of starting from scratch, partners and customer can now:

  • Reuse proven integration patterns
  • Accelerate ICSM-IRT implementations
  • Focus on value‑adding extensions rather than plumbing
  • Align with SAP‑recommended architecture and standards

The content is intentionally modular, making it easier to adapt to different IRT systems or extend with additional scenarios.

What's Included

The published package SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management-Sample Integration with IRT demonstrates end-to-end integration scenarios across both planning and operations, based on GS1 Clinical Trials standards (3.6) and SAP-recommended integration patterns.

How to Consume the Accelerator Content

This can be consumed using SAP’s standard process for pre-packaged integration content: The pre-packaged integration content in SAP integration Suite is a set of Integration flows (iflows) leveraging the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to integrate IRT and SAP ICSM smoothly. This prepackage content takes care of many aspects of integration; some of these are as follows:

  1. Connect the IRT system to SAP Integration Suite artifacts via API management.
  2. Transformation of message payload from GS1 to ICSM and vice versa.
  3. Seamless integration of SAP Event Mesh to take an action against the events raised from ICSM (e.g. sales order created; Inventory report executed etc.).

This pre-package integration content supports the following interfaces for planning and operation in ICSM:

Interfaces for ICSM Planning

  1. Study Information
  2. IRT actuals and Master

Interfaces for ICSM Operations

  1. Inventory Report
  2. Shipment request and confirmation
  3. Dispatch Advice
  4. Receiving Advice
  5. Dispensation Advice
  6. Kit Status Change (Inbound/Outbound)

Below are the steps to consume the pre-packaged integration content:

Step 1 - Discover the package

Navigate to your SAP Integration Suite - Integration - Discover tab and search for the package "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management Integration with IRT"

Below is the diagram shows the navigation steps.

                                                                                                              Fig: Integration Suite

Select the package and click copy to move it into your design workspace.  

Note: As with all standard packages, once you copy it, modifications in your workplace do not auto-inherit future SAP updates- SAP recommends keeping custom changes separate.

Step 2 - Review the Artifacts

Review each artifact integration flows and configure the user credentials for ICSM APIs and OAuth2 Client Credentials for event mesh using its service key values.

Step 3 - Configure the defined externalized parameters in each artifact

Update the values in each of the defined externalized parameters in each of the artifact integration flows.

The integration flows combine API-based and Event-driven approaches using SAP Integration Suite, SAP API Management, SAP Event Mesh and Cloud Connector, reflecting a reference architecture suitable for real-world landscapes.

Reference, not a Black Box

This is sample/accelerator content. Its purpose is to illustrate integration approaches and provide reusable building blocks - not to replace product features.

As with other SAP community assets, it does not come with the same lifecycle guarantees or support rigor as standard SAP product functionality. Instead, it is meant to enable the ecosystem to innovate, customize, and deliver differentiated solutions on top of SAP ICSM.

Get Started

  • Explore the sample Integration Package on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
  • Review the ICSM-IRT Integration architecture and patterns.
  • Copy, adapt and extend the content for your own scenarios.

If you are an IRT vendor, Partner or Customer working with ICSM, then this is an opportunity to reduce the integration effort and contribute to a growing, interoperable ecosystem around clinical supply management.

This Accelerator Content lays out the groundwork for real-world ICSM-IRT integrations by providing pre-built, GS1 aligned, event driven flows that can be directly adopted for the proof-of-concept work. 

In the upcoming blog of this series, this accelerator content forms the technical backbone for validating the end-to-end data exchange between ICSM and an IRT platform, which accelerates onboarding IRT partners and helps to reduce the custom development effort.

Engage with us

If you are interested in learning more about SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management, please consider following the new "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management".

We are happy to receive your feedback, questions or thoughts about SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management in a comment.

entry.title: Sales Order Print Output Log S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We have requirement to export PDF string for sales order confirmation. As standard sales order API doesn't provide PDF xstring we are thing of using API API_CLOUD_PRINT_PULL_SRV to pull directly from print queues but challenge here is we are unable to fetch relation between technical ID displayed here in message log and sales order number.

Does anyone know other API which can provide this data to establish link between printer API and sales order number.

Thanks

entry.title: Equivalence between classic extractors and CDS views
entry.content:
Hi all.
Why SAP hasn't publish a Note, document, whatever, that explain clear and concisely an equivalence between classic BW extractors and CDS views?
It´s a complete nosense to search through thousands of pages (notes, posts...), one by one extractor, if there is a CDS View that offers the same.
For example, consider 2LIS_02_ITM: SAP Note 2500202 tell us the extractor is "Whitelisted; DS working - no restrictions" (and no more information). However, we can find the C_PurchaseOrderItemDEX CDS View, that is "available for all external consumers who want to extract the data to SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) or other external systems.". So, can we replace 2LIS_02_ITM with C_PurchaseOrderItemDEX or not? Should we check field by field, join by join? What about a migration with cents of extractors?

Customers have invested tones of time and money developing and enhancing their ECC and BW sytems and deserves that.

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP: Last Period Aggregation with Example
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Not sure if this is old post and currently as of today, we can use IBP_LPA that will show the last period even if the technical week is split as explained above eg.  TW 09a and 09b, it will pick up the value in TW 09b if IBP_LPA is coded in calculation sheet.

eg.

ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@REQUEST = SUM("ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@WKPRODLOCNOUOMTO")

ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@CALWKPROD = SUM("ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@WKPRODLOC")

ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@WKPRODLOCUOMTO = "UOMCONVERSIONFACTOR@PRODUOMTO" * "ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@WKPRODLOC"

ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@WKPRODLOCNOUOMTO = IBP_LPA("ZINVENTORYTARGETVAL@WKPRODLOCUOMTO")

entry.title: Service Catalog - Long text mass upload (ML01/ML02/ML03 Editor)
entry.content:
Upload Long Text for Service Catalog (ML01/ML02/ML03) using ABAP – Complete Guide with Working Solution

 Introduction

Uploading Long Text (Lg Text) for Service Catalog in SAP (Transactions ML01 / ML02 / ML03) is not straightforward.

Even if you use SAVE_TEXT, you may face issues like:

  • Only the first line gets updated

  • Remaining lines are ignored

  • Text appears incorrectly in ML03

  • No clear error message

👉 The root cause is not your code, but a hidden SAP design detail that is rarely documented.

This blog explains everything end-to-end:

  • Data model

  • Common mistakes

  • Root cause analysis

  • Fully working ABAP solution

⚠️ Prerequisite

Before uploading Long Text, ensure:

✔ Service Catalog already exists
✔ Service lines are present in the system
✔ Entries are available in table ESLZ

🔍 Why this is mandatory?

Long Text is linked with:

  • Service Type (LBNUM)

  • Edition (AUSGB)

  • Line (ZEILE)

👉 If ZEILE does not exist in ESLZ, the text:

  • Will not be visible in ML03

  • May get stored but not linked

🧠 Understanding the Data Model

1️⃣ ESLZ Table (Structure Only)

  • Stores service lines

  • Contains hierarchy (Block, Sub-lines)

  • DOES NOT store long text

2️⃣ Long Text Storage

Long Text is stored using SAPscript:

Table Purpose

STXHText Header
STXLText Lines

🔥 Text Key Mapping

Field Value

TDOBJECTESLZ
TDIDLTXT
TDNAMELBNUM + AUSGB + ZEILE

📊 Example Scenario

Excel Input

Service Edition Line Block TDLINE

001200400000000040000000004Test abc 4
001200400000000050000000004Test abc 5
001200400000000060000000004Test abc 6

Expected Output in ML03

Line 4 → Test abc 4  
Line 5 → Test abc 5  
Line 6 → Test abc 6

❌ Common Mistake (Root Cause)

Many implementations use:

GROUP BY lbnumm ausgb blknr.

Why this fails?

  • BLKNR = Block level

  • SAP stores text at LINE level (ZEILE)

Result:

❌ Only first text visible
❌ Remaining lines ignored
❌ Incorrect mapping in ML03

💥 Root Cause Explained

SAP Service Catalog Long Text is maintained per LINE (ZEILE), not per BLOCK (BLKNR)

✅ Correct Approach

✔ Process data line-wise (ZEILE)
✔ Generate text key using ZEILE
✔ Avoid grouping by BLKNR

💻 Complete Working ABAP Code

Below is a complete ABAP report which program to upload Service catalog long text, using Excel file upload.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZPR_SERVICE_CAT_UPLOAD
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zpr_service_cat_upload.


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* TYPES
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_excel,
         lbnumm   TYPE eslz-lbnum,
         ausgb    TYPE eslz-ausgb,
         zeile    TYPE eslz-zeile, "added on 23rd march
         sortn    TYPE eslz-sortn, "added on 23rd march
         blknr    TYPE eslz-blknr,
         meins    TYPE eslz-meins, "added on 23rd march
         tdformat TYPE tline-tdformat,
         tdline   TYPE tline-tdline,
         seq      TYPE i,
       END OF ty_excel.

DATA: gt_excel TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel,
      gs_excel TYPE ty_excel.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* RESULT STRUCTURE
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_result,
         icon    TYPE icon_d,
         lbnumm  TYPE eslz-lbnum,
         status  TYPE char10,
         message TYPE string,
       END OF ty_result.

DATA: gt_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_result,
      gs_result TYPE ty_result.

CONSTANTS:
  gc_sheet_exclude TYPE string VALUE 'Sheet1'.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* SELECTION SCREEN
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE string OBLIGATORY.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* F4 HELP
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.

  DATA: lt_filetab TYPE filetable,
        lv_rc      TYPE i.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_open_dialog
    CHANGING
      file_table = lt_filetab
      rc         = lv_rc.

  IF lv_rc > 0.
    READ TABLE lt_filetab INTO DATA(ls_file) INDEX 1.
    p_file = ls_file-filename.
  ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* START-OF-SELECTION
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
START-OF-SELECTION.

  PERFORM read_excel.
  PERFORM upload_texts.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* READ EXCEL
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM read_excel.

  DATA: lo_excel         TYPE REF TO cl_fdt_xl_spreadsheet,
        lv_xstring       TYPE xstring,
        lt_raw           TYPE solix_tab,
        lv_len           TYPE i,
        lv_worksheetname TYPE string,
        lt_worksheets    TYPE string_table,
        lo_data          TYPE REF TO data.

* Upload file
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_UPLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename   = p_file
      filetype   = 'BIN'
    IMPORTING
      filelength = lv_len
    TABLES
      data_tab   = lt_raw.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      input_length = lv_len
    IMPORTING
      buffer       = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab   = lt_raw.

* Create Excel object
  TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT lo_excel
        EXPORTING
          document_name = p_file
          xdocument     = lv_xstring.
    CATCH cx_fdt_excel_core.
      MESSAGE 'Error reading Excel file' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_worksheet_names(
    IMPORTING worksheet_names = lt_worksheets ).

  DELETE lt_worksheets WHERE table_line = gc_sheet_exclude.

  READ TABLE lt_worksheets INTO lv_worksheetname INDEX 1.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Worksheet not found' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  lo_data = lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_itab_from_worksheet(
               worksheet_name = lv_worksheetname ).

* Convert to internal table
  CALL FUNCTION 'Z_XLS_INTO_ITAB'
    EXPORTING
      header      = 'X'
      str         = 'ZTS_SC_EXCEL'
      iv_data_ref = lo_data
      hdr_len     = '1'
    IMPORTING
      it_xls_data = gt_excel.

  IF gt_excel IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'No data found in Excel file' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* UPLOAD TEXTS
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM upload_texts.

  DATA: lt_grouped TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ty_excel
                      WITH UNIQUE KEY lbnumm ausgb blknr,
        ls_group   TYPE ty_excel.


* Sort properly
  SORT gt_excel BY lbnumm ausgb blknr seq.

** Grouping loop (modern syntax)
*  SORT gt_excel BY lbnumm ausgb blknr seq.

"commented on 23.03

*  LOOP AT gt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel)
*       GROUP BY ( lbnumm = ls_excel-lbnumm
*                  ausgb  = ls_excel-ausgb
*                  zeile  = ls_excel-zeile
*                  blknr  = ls_excel-blknr )
*       ASCENDING
*       ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<group>).
*
*    DATA: lt_lines       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tline,
*          lt_group_lines TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel,
*          ls_header      TYPE thead.
*
**--- Build NAME (with ALPHA)
*    DATA(lv_lbnumm) = |{ <group>-lbnumm ALPHA = IN }|.
*    DATA(lv_ausgb)  = <group>-ausgb.
*    DATA(lv_blknr)  = |{ <group>-blknr ALPHA = IN }|.
*
**    DATA(lv_name) = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_blknr.
*    DATA lv_name TYPE thead-tdname.
*
*    lv_name = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_blknr.
*
**--- Step 1: Move group data into internal table
*    lt_group_lines = VALUE #( FOR wa IN GROUP <group> ( wa ) ).
*
**--- Step 2: Sort by SEQ (VERY IMPORTANT)
*    SORT lt_group_lines BY seq.
*
**--- Step 3: Prepare ALL lines
*    CLEAR lt_lines.
*
*    LOOP AT lt_group_lines INTO DATA(ls_line_data).
*
*      IF ls_line_data-tdline IS NOT INITIAL.
*        APPEND VALUE tline(
*          tdformat = ls_line_data-tdformat                     " <-- FIX
*          tdline   = ls_line_data-tdline ) TO lt_lines.
*      ENDIF.
*
*    ENDLOOP.
*
**--- Header
*    ls_header = VALUE thead(
*        tdobject = 'ESLZ'
*        tdname   = lv_name
*        tdid     = 'LTXT'
*        tdspras  = sy-langu ).
*
*
*
**--- Save text (overwrite mode)
*    CALL FUNCTION 'SAVE_TEXT'
*      EXPORTING
*        header = ls_header
**       insert = space
*        insert = space
*       savemode_direct = 'X'
*      TABLES
*        lines  = lt_lines
*      EXCEPTIONS
*        OTHERS = 1.
*
**--- Result handling
*    DATA(ls_result) = VALUE ty_result(
*        lbnumm = <group>-lbnumm ).
*
*    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*
*      DATA lv_msg TYPE string.
*
*      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
*        INTO lv_msg
*        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
*
*      ls_result-status  = 'ERROR'.
*      ls_result-message = lv_msg.
*      ls_result-icon    = '@5C@'.
*
*    ELSE.
*
*
**      CALL FUNCTION 'COMMIT_TEXT'
**        EXPORTING
**          object           = ls_header-tdobject
**          name             = ls_header-tdname
**          id               = ls_header-tdid
**          language         = sy-langu
***         savemode_direct  = abap_true.
*          ls_result-status = 'SUCCESS'.
*      ls_result-message = 'Text Saved Successfully'.
*      ls_result-icon    = '@5B@'.
*
*    ENDIF.
*
*    APPEND ls_result TO gt_result.
*
*  ENDLOOP.
*
*  COMMIT WORK.

"commented on 23.03

  DATA: lt_lines  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tline,
        ls_header TYPE thead,
        ls_result TYPE ty_result.

  LOOP AT gt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel).

*--- Build TEXT NAME using ZEILE (IMPORTANT FIX)
    DATA(lv_lbnumm) = |{ ls_excel-lbnumm ALPHA = IN }|.
    DATA(lv_ausgb)  = ls_excel-ausgb.
    DATA(lv_zeile)  = |{ ls_excel-zeile ALPHA = IN }|.

    DATA(lv_name) = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_zeile.

*--- Prepare text lines (single line per ZEILE)
    CLEAR lt_lines.

    IF ls_excel-tdline IS NOT INITIAL.
      APPEND VALUE tline(
        tdformat = ls_excel-tdformat
        tdline   = ls_excel-tdline ) TO lt_lines.
    ENDIF.

*--- Header
    ls_header = VALUE thead(
      tdobject = 'ESLZ'
      tdname   = lv_name
      tdid     = 'LTXT'
      tdspras  = sy-langu ).

*--- Clear result
    CLEAR ls_result.
    ls_result-lbnumm = ls_excel-lbnumm.

*--- SAVE TEXT
    CALL FUNCTION 'SAVE_TEXT'
      EXPORTING
        header          = ls_header
        insert          = space
        savemode_direct = 'X'
      TABLES
        lines           = lt_lines
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS          = 1.

*--- Handle result
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.

      DATA: lv_msg TYPE string.

      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
        INTO lv_msg
        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

      ls_result-status  = 'ERROR'.
      ls_result-message = lv_msg.
      ls_result-icon    = '@5C@'.

    ELSE.

      ls_result-status  = 'SUCCESS'.
      ls_result-message = |Text saved for line { ls_excel-zeile }|.
      ls_result-icon    = '@5B@'.

    ENDIF.

    APPEND ls_result TO gt_result.

  ENDLOOP.

*--- Final commit
  COMMIT WORK.

  PERFORM display_alv.


ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* DISPLAY ALV
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display_alv.

  DATA: lt_fieldcat TYPE slis_t_fieldcat_alv,
        ls_fieldcat TYPE slis_fieldcat_alv,
        ls_layout   TYPE slis_layout_alv.

* ICON
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'ICON'.
  ls_fieldcat-icon      = 'X'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Status'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* LBNUM
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'LBNUMM'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Package'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* STATUS
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'STATUS'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Status'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* MESSAGE
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MESSAGE'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Message'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  ls_layout-zebra = 'X'.

  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
    EXPORTING
      i_callback_program = sy-repid
      is_layout          = ls_layout
      it_fieldcat        = lt_fieldcat
    TABLES
      t_outtab           = gt_result.

ENDFORM.  


Function Module to read Excel sheet into internal table - 
FUNCTION z_xls_into_itab.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(P_FILE) TYPE  RLGRAP-FILENAME OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(HEADER) TYPE  CHAR1 DEFAULT 'X'
*"     REFERENCE(STR) TYPE  CHAR30 OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(IV_DATA_REF) TYPE REF TO  DATA OPTIONAL
*"     VALUE(HDR_LEN) TYPE  SYST_TABIX OPTIONAL
*"  EXPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IT_XLS_DATA) TYPE  STANDARD TABLE
*"  TABLES
*"      T_FILE STRUCTURE  ALSMEX_TABLINE OPTIONAL
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  TYPE-POOLS: abap,slis.
  FIELD-SYMBOLS:<dy_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
   <fs_current_field>,
   <wa_str>,<wa_temp>
  .
  DATA:new_line      TYPE REF TO data,
       new_line2     TYPE REF TO data,
       li_fieldcat   TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
       dy_table      TYPE REF TO data,
* t_file      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF alsmex_tabline,
       wa_file       TYPE alsmex_tabline,
       v_col         TYPE i,
       begin_row     TYPE i,
       zls_alsm_data TYPE zts_alsmex_tabline.

********This will provide Fields in Row format of Structure created(which should be similar to Excel Format).
  CALL FUNCTION 'LVC_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE'
    EXPORTING
      i_structure_name       = str
    CHANGING
      ct_fieldcat            = li_fieldcat
    EXCEPTIONS
      inconsistent_interface = 1
      program_error          = 2
      OTHERS                 = 3.

  IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

********This will create internal table which will have fields as per the Excel Format.
    CALL METHOD cl_alv_table_create=>create_dynamic_table
      EXPORTING
        it_fieldcatalog           = li_fieldcat
        i_length_in_byte          = 'X'
      IMPORTING
        ep_table                  = dy_table
      EXCEPTIONS
        generate_subpool_dir_full = 1
        OTHERS                    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0.
      ASSIGN dy_table->* TO <dy_table>.
    ENDIF.

******V_col will hold the number of Columns inside Excel File which is later passed to ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE’ .

    DESCRIBE TABLE li_fieldcat LINES v_col.

    LOOP AT t_file INTO wa_file.

******This Block will create new area to store column wise data.<wa_temp> acts as a work area which is completely blank at start of new row
      AT NEW row.
        CREATE DATA new_line2 LIKE LINE OF <dy_table>.
        ASSIGN new_line2->* TO <wa_temp>.
      ENDAT.

*******for every iteration, wa_file-col will have increment till number of columns in Excel file and then reset to 1 when there’s new row i.e row = 1. (Refer example at end of blog)

      ASSIGN COMPONENT wa_file-col OF STRUCTURE <wa_temp> TO <fs_current_field>.
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0.

        <fs_current_field> = wa_file-value.
        UNASSIGN <fs_current_field>.
      ENDIF.

*****Finally once all Columns are processed and new row is about to start, append the workarea into Internal table

      AT END OF row.
        APPEND <wa_temp> TO it_xls_data.
        UNASSIGN <fs_current_field>.
        UNASSIGN <wa_temp>.
      ENDAT.

    ENDLOOP.
*ENDIF.

***Mapping IV_DATA_REF (Excel data) to Fields

*-----------------------FIELD SYMBOLS----------------------------------*
    FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lfs_data_tab> TYPE ANY TABLE,
                   <fs_1>         TYPE any,
                   <fs_2>         TYPE any.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
    hdr_len = COND #( WHEN hdr_len IS INITIAL THEN 1 ELSE hdr_len ).

    ASSIGN iv_data_ref->* TO <lfs_data_tab>.

    IF <lfs_data_tab> IS ASSIGNED AND <dy_table> IS ASSIGNED.

      LOOP AT <lfs_data_tab> ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_data>).
        IF sy-tabix LE hdr_len.
          CONTINUE.  "Skip line 1
        ENDIF.

        CREATE DATA new_line2 LIKE LINE OF <dy_table>.
        ASSIGN new_line2->* TO <wa_temp>.

        DO 250 TIMES.
          ASSIGN COMPONENT sy-index OF STRUCTURE <lfs_data> TO <fs_1>.
          ASSIGN COMPONENT sy-index OF STRUCTURE <wa_temp> TO <fs_2>.
          READ TABLE li_fieldcat INTO DATA(fs_fieldcat) INDEX sy-index .
          IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL AND fs_fieldcat-datatype = 'DATS'.
            IF <fs_1> IS ASSIGNED AND <fs_2> IS ASSIGNED AND strlen( <fs_1> ) EQ 10 .
              DATA(lv_date) = |{ <fs_1>(4) }{ <fs_1>+5(2) }{ <fs_1>+8(2) }| .
              CONDENSE lv_date.
              <fs_2> = lv_date.
*              <fs_2> = <fs_1>.
              UNASSIGN: <fs_1>, <fs_2>.
              CLEAR: lv_date.
            ENDIF.
          ELSE.
            IF <fs_1> IS ASSIGNED AND <fs_2> IS ASSIGNED.
              <fs_2> = <fs_1>.
              UNASSIGN: <fs_1>, <fs_2>.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDDO.

        IF <wa_temp> IS ASSIGNED AND <wa_temp> IS NOT INITIAL.
*          MOVE-CORRESPONDING <wa_temp> TO it_xls_data.
          APPEND <wa_temp> TO it_xls_data.
        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

ENDFUNCTION.

⚠️ Important Points to Remember

  • ❌ Do NOT use BLKNR for text key

  • ✅ Always use ZEILE

  • ❌ Avoid INSERT = 'X'

  • ✅ Use INSERT = SPACE for overwrite

  • ✅ Ensure ESLZ entries exist

🧪 Debug Tip

Use function module READ_TEXT:

OBJECT = 'ESLZ'
ID     = 'LTXT'
NAME   = '001200400000000004'

👉 Helps verify actual stored text

Final Result

✔ All lines updated correctly
✔ Correct mapping in ML03
✔ No missing or overwritten data

 Conclusion

Uploading Service Catalog Long Text becomes easy once you understand:

  • SAP stores text per LINE (ZEILE)

  • Not per BLOCK (BLKNR)

  • Correct text key is the key to success

💡 Key Takeaway

If your long text upload is not working — you are most likely using the wrong key.

🙌 Closing Note

This is a common issue with no clear documentation.
Hope this blog saves you hours of debugging.

Feel free to share your feedback or improvements 🚀

entry.title: Re: CALLING SAP HANA STORED PROCEDURES WITH VARIABLES
entry.content:
Hi,

Not 100% sure about the exact syntax for HANA, but try something like this:

/* select * from OCRD c */
/* select * from OITM i */
DECLARE CardCode NVARCHAR(50);
DECLARE ItemCode NVARCHAR(50);
CardCode := /* c.CardCode */ '[%0]';
ItemCode := /* i.ItemCode */ '[%1]';

CALL MY_PROC(CardCode, ItemCode);

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: Is it possible to connect two XI adapters to the same SAP backend (PI and Integration Suite)?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently evaluating a migration from SAP PI/PO to the SAP Integration Suite.

In our current setup, several interfaces use ABAP proxies via the XI adapter.

During the transition phase we are wondering:

  • Is it technically possible to connect two XI adapter endpoints to the same SAP backend system as sender and receiver?
    • one pointing to PI/PO
    • one pointing to Integration Suite

Questions:

  1. Can an SAP backend system maintain two XI adapter connections simultaneously?
  2. How would the proxy decide which endpoint to use (logical port, configuration, etc.)?
  3. Are there any best practices or limitations for this setup during migration?

Any experiences or recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thanks

Dear Experts,

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI platform taking considerable t...
entry.content: I'd probably recommend creating a case for this. There's no feasible reason that should take > 2 hours. It's probably the command that is supposed to be executed is stuck, we've seen similar issues in the past. Some of the commands can take a long time if they rely on a temporary CMS, for example AddNode and Deployfiles scripts start a temporary CMS and login. If there's an issue during the login there's a period of waiting where we are persistently trying to logon - but no waiting period I'm aware of that should take > 2 hours.

entry.title: Re: Questions About Display Options in the New SAP Succes...
entry.content:
Hi @Joe0603 

1. The category is only editable if you access the Edit Goal (Edit Target from your screenshot), but otherwise, it shows under the Goal Name in the side panel. The reasoning behind might be on just having the most common actions done on the form (rating, adding comments, creating an activity...) but for other attributes, like metric, category or weight, the goal should be opened on the edit mode, which can be easily done on the form.

As per your question "Is there a way to display the category in view mode in the new UI?", if you would like to enforce the category to be read-only even when editing the goal, this is possible with Goal Plan states. If you set up a restriction on the control-category-element field, you could make the category read-only or even not visible when the goal plan has a specific status. The change of goal plan state can be done in the Goal Plan itself or when the form moves to a specific step. 

2072109 - Configuring & Using Goal Plan State in SuccessFactors Goal Management https://me.sap.com/notes/2072109/E

Additionally, in 1H 2026 release there will be a new Mass Change Goal Plan States tool where administrators can update multiple goal plan statuses.

2. In Legacy Form experience, you could use this code below, and according to the KBA 3669517 - Cannot hide Rating Scale Label Link in Performance Form Summary section https://me.sap.com/notes/3669517/E  this should be also supported in Latest Experience next 1H 2026 release.  What I am not 100% if this would also be applicable to Goal and Attribute sections, since the icon on the Summary looks different, although they have the same purpose to display the rating scale levels and descriptions.

<field-permission type="none">
<role-name>*</role-name>
<field refid="rating-scale-label-link"></field>
<route-step stepid="*"/>
</field-permission>

3.  Are there field restrictions on the calculated overall rating? I wonder if you could display that rating on the calculated rating field and you can hide or even disable the manual overall rating. Does it show different results? 

Hope these will be helpful,

Thanks!

Jordi

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence in Organizational Change Management: From Theory to Practice
entry.content:
As a quick recap: in our previous post, we explored the impact of AI across our six

Organizational Change Management (OCM) dimensions:  In our previous post (read the article here), we explored HOW AI impacts the six OCM dimensions.

Now let's get practical: WHAT can you do with it - now?

Based on conversations with change professionals across industries, we've identified three high-impact opportunities where AI is already delivering results in SAP transformations.

Being in mutual exchange with other change professionals, we have identified many opportunities for the improvement of transformation & change projects by using AI capabilities to solve several challenges.

From streamlining content creation to enabling impactful communication on tight budgets and acting as a strategic sparring partner, AI opens new possibilities for tackling challenges and driving innovation in OCM. Below, you’ll find a graphic that presents the three opportunities at a glance.

 

 

 

 

 

1st Opportunity – Transforming Content Creation with AI

Scenario
Your teams produce great ideas—but turning workshop outcomes into on‑brand, stakeholder‑ready assets is slow, manual, inconsistent, and scattered across channels. Deadlines stack up, style guides get bent, and version control becomes a guessing game. You need a way to turn raw inputs into professional, audience‑specific materials without the heavy lifting.

AI Solution
Use generative AI to turn workshop outputs, briefs, and project artifacts into on‑brand deliverables: vision statements, key messages, stakeholder briefings, enablement outlines, and even first‑cut engagement plans. Configure brand and tone guardrails, map stakeholder preferences, and let AI adapt content by audience, channel, and language —reducing formatting work while improving consistency.

Real Impact
You can move from raw inputs to polished, customer‑specific content faster, keep materials aligned to your brand and narrative, and tailor messages to stakeholder needs with less manual effort. Content creation becomes a coordinated flow across change strategy, communication, and execution—supporting momentum throughout the program.

Getting Started
Collect a small set of canonical inputs (brand guidelines, message house, workshop notes, key slides) and define master prompts and templates for each asset type. Pilot with two priority deliverables—such as a vision statement and a stakeholder briefing—keep a human review step for accuracy and tone and expand to additional channels once the workflow proves reliable.

 

2nd Opportunity – Realizing appealing communication with tight budgets 

Scenario
The plan is solid, the milestones are set—but the budget for agencies and production support is too expensive. You still need a steady cadence of videos, posts, articles, and emails that land with different audiences and regions. The choice shouldn’t be “go quiet” or “burn out the team.”

AI Solution
Use generative AI to transform SAP standard content and your project artifacts (charters, release notes, FAQs, town‑hall transcripts) into ready‑to‑use assets—short explainer videos, community posts, newsletters, and stakeholder emails. Apply brand and tone guardrails, translate or reuse for key markets, and generate channel‑specific variants with approvals routed to your team.

Real Impact
You can maintain a consistent communications drumbeat without additional headcount, reach more audiences in their preferred channels and languages, and keep messages aligned to project milestones—preserving quality while staying within budget.

Getting Started
Identify your source content and priority channels, define brand/voice prompts and approval rules, and pilot two to three asset types (e.g., a monthly newsletter, a short video, and a stakeholder email). Keep human review for accuracy and context, then expand once the workflow feels reliable.

 

3rd Opportunity – Engage AI as your OCM sparring partner

Scenario
You’ve got strong change ideas, but not always the time—or the bench—to test them, iterate fast, and pressure‑test assumptions. Workshops end, momentum fades, and decisions wait for the next availability slot. You need a trusted counterpart that can challenge thinking and turn concepts into actionable plans on demand.

AI Solution
Set up an AI assistant grounded in your internal playbooks, past project lessons, and SAP good practices. Use it to iterate on change narratives, draft stakeholder maps and engagement plans, compare scenario options, summarize feedback patterns, and propose next‑best actions—available on demand, 24/7.

Real Impact
You can move from idea to plan faster, explore more options before committing, and document the rationale behind decisions. The assistant augments your expertise with rapid synthesis and structured recommendations, while you retain judgment and sign‑off.

Getting Started
Curate a safe, high‑quality knowledge base (frameworks, project docs, templates, lessons learned), define clear prompts and review criteria, and begin with co‑drafting tasks such as sponsor briefings or comms outlines. Validate outputs with your team, then extend to scenario testing and decision support as confidence grows.

Looking ahead

AI brings new momentum to how we deliver change—but its impact depends on how intentionally we choose to work with it. Start small with a clearly scoped use case, define success metrics, and iterate based on evidence. Keep experts in the loop throughout: AI accelerates first drafts, clustering, and signal detection, while humans ensure narrative, nuance, and sound decision‑making. Establish privacy, security, and responsible‑AI guardrails from day one to build trust and transparency.

But most importantly: don’t wait for “perfect AI.”
Experiment, learn, adapt—and share your experiences. Your insights help the wider change community move forward faster.

✓ Start with ONE high-pain use case (e.g., stakeholder analysis automation)

✓ Define success metrics upfront (time saved, quality scores, adoption lift)

✓ Keep humans in the loop: AI drafts, experts review and decide

✓ Establish guardrails: privacy, security, bias checks—non-negotiable from day one

We should not be waiting for perfect AI—keep experimenting, learning, and iterating.

If you want to go deeper or need support setting up your first AI‑powered OCM workflow, reach out via maja.polke@gmail.com or hannah.bruelke@gmail.com or explore our SharePoint site for templates, guides, and examples.

Together, let’s shape how AI elevates OCM—today and for the future.

entry.title: Re: Governing the Emerging Collective Intelligence
entry.content:
Thank you,  @sandiago, I really appreciate your thoughtful perspective. I especially agree with your point on the shift from managing data to governing intelligence — that feels like a fundamental transition we’re starting to see across enterprise systems. A strong data foundation is clearly essential, as you mentioned. It defines what the system considers “truth.” It will be interesting to see how this evolves — especially as customers begin combining their own data platforms, models, and agents with SAP’s applications. That’s where governance becomes not just a control mechanism, but a shared responsibility across the ecosystem.

I’m planning to continue exploring this topic further and go deeper into how governance needs to evolve in this context — really appreciate the inspiration your comment adds to that direction.

entry.title: Unlock Enterprise-Scale Integration and Automation with SAP: A 5-Part Webcast Series
entry.content:
Running integration at enterprise scale requires more than connecting systems - it demands a unified strategy, consistent governance, and a strong operational foundation. To help organizations navigate this complex landscape, we’re excited to announce a deep-dive 5-part webcast series that shows how SAP Integration Suite and SAP Business Technology Platform can serve as a true enterprise-wide integration platform.

Whether you’re looking to simplify complexity, strengthen governance, or enable future-ready automation, this series provides a structured path from vision to sustainable execution. Here’s a breakdown of what you can expect from each session:

 

1. Rethinking Integration: Building a Unified Enterprise-wide Platform

Integration starts with consolidation and unification. This session explores how organizations can position SAP Integration Suite as a central platform, connecting systems, processes, and data across the enterprise. Learn strategies to streamline your integration landscape and create a foundation for scalable, enterprise-wide operations.

Register here:  https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661068&sourcepage=register

 

2. From Vision to Execution: Building a Scalable Integration Strategy

Turning integration vision into reality requires a deliberate, structured approach. This session dives into the steps organizations can take to develop a scalable integration strategy that aligns business goals with technology execution. Discover best practices for governance, architecture, and execution that drive measurable value.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5256733&sessionid=1&key=2F2D713929E44EC97A0255F0674D0915&groupId=6661111&sourcepage=register

 

3. Achieve Enterprise-grade Operations for SAP Integration Suite

Operational excellence is critical to sustaining integration at scale. In this session, we focus on how to strengthen governance, monitor performance, and build resilient processes with SAP Integration Suite. Learn how enterprise-grade operations ensure consistency, reliability, and scalability across your integration landscape.

Regiser here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259511&sessionid=1&key=905F0343E0BA503A283C97B3645BCB31&groupId=6667225&sourcepage=register

 

4. Drive Intelligent Business Transformation with Agentic Enterprise Automation

Intelligent business transformation requires more than isolated automation - it demands connected, adaptive processes powered by AI. Explore how SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Business Transformation Management enable agentic enterprise automation at scale. Learn how to design, govern, and measure AI agents that drive true process transformation, generating measurable outcomes across complex enterprise environments.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5259684&sessionid=1&key=10935CA2537B96624DD065F7EA514C2F&groupId=6661382&sourcepage=register

 

5. Develop Robust Automations with SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite

Agentic enterprise automation only succeeds when systems, workflows, and applications are seamlessly connected. This session demonstrates how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite work together to enable end-to-end automation across SAP and non-SAP landscapes. See live demos, customer success stories, and practical examples showing how low-code development and enterprise-grade integration accelerate automation while maintaining control, visibility, and scalability.

Register here: https://event.on24.com/eventRegistration/EventLobbyServlet?target=reg20.jsp&eventid=5258498&sessionid=1&key=76AD4CD903C206A20A2CFFE8270CC25C&groupId=6661286&sourcepage=register

 

This series is designed for integration architects, IT leaders, and business transformation professionals who want to take their enterprise integration and automation initiatives to the next level. Join us to learn how SAP can help you simplify complexity, achieve operational excellence, and unlock intelligent, future-ready automation.

entry.title: Administering Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
Why Administration Matters

Before organizations can effectively manage sustainability data, they need a solid technical foundation. SAP Sustainability Control Tower requires proper setup, role management, and system integration to ensure accurate, consistent, and scalable operations.

A well-administered system enables:

  • Reliable data integration across SAP landscapes
  • Secure and structured user access
  • Smooth operation for business users
  • A strong foundation for reporting and steering sustainability

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This compact, 1.5-hour course focuses on the essential administrative tasks required to get SAP Sustainability Control Tower up and running:

  1. Setting Up Accounts and Services
    Learn how to manage accounts and services to prepare the system for use.
  2. Assigning Roles
    Understand different user roles and how to assign them to ensure proper access control and governance.
  3. Configuring System Integration
    Connect SAP Sustainability Control Tower with key systems, including:
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Datasphere
  • SAP EHS Management
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • SAP Analytics Cloud

This ensures seamless data exchange and a fully connected sustainability landscape.

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Reporting and Steering Sustainability in Your Businesses using SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • System Administrators responsible for setup and configuration

Build the technical foundation for effective sustainability management and start the Learning Journey “Administering SAP Sustainability Control Tower” here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to change field Name_org2 in Mass transactio...
entry.content:
Most likely the field is set to display-only in the screen configuration for that transaction. Check your authorization objects first, missing change permissions on that field will block edits without any clear error message. If it was editable before, a recent transport may have changed the config.

What is the exact transaction and is the field greyed out or just not saving?

entry.title: How to optimize Requirements Engineering with CENIT and SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
Welcome to this blog post! In today’s edition, I want to outline how you can efficiently capture and evaluate requirements in a structured way and process them collaboratively across company boundaries, leveraging SAP Integrated Product Development as Digital Product Platform and the APP RVC (Requirements Valuation Cockpit) from CENIT AG.

No time to read? Register to access the recordings of our two Meet the expert webinars via the SAP Supply Chain Management Enablement Hub.

In this blog, you will find out how OEMs and suppliers can

  • Jointly work on requirements
  • Create transparency for all stakeholders
  • Achieve faster and safer results through a shared understanding of product and quality.
  • Work and share specifications

Moreover, we will discuss how design and product teams can work together to develop digital product structures, formulations and recipes – from the initial concept all the way to the final production.

A core part of this journey is SAP Integrated Product Development, our solution designed to help you define, develop, and deliver sustainable products by connecting insights, processes, and people across your enterprise. While SAP provides the powerful digital backbone for this process, the true magic often happens when we leverage the deep industry expertise of our partners. Today, I want to highlight a great example of this synergy by looking at how our partner, CENIT, is helping companies optimize one of the most critical phases of product development: requirements engineering.


The Challenge: Taming the Complexity of Early-Stage Development

Every great product begins with well-defined requirements. But as anyone in R&D or PLM knows, this "fuzzy front end" can be a chaotic mix of customer requests, supplier inputs, regulatory constraints, and internal specifications. Managing this process often involves manual document handling, endless email chains, and a constant struggle to ensure the right experts are evaluating the right information in a timely manner.

This complexity can lead to delays, misinterpretations, and missed opportunities. How can you quickly assess the feasibility of a customer request? How do you efficiently collaborate with external suppliers on technical specifications? And how do you ensure you aren't re-inventing the wheel for a problem your team has already solved?

[The "Requirements Engineering" process flow. This diagram illustrates the complex web of interactions between customers, internal departments, and suppliers.]

 

The Solution: CENIT's Requirements Valuation Cockpit (RVC)

This is where CENITs APP, the Requirements Valuation Cockpit (RVC), comes in. It’s an intelligent evaluation platform designed to bring order to the chaos of requirements management, acting as a powerful addon for SAP IPD.

The RVC provides a set of features or "accelerators" that automate and simplify key steps in the process.

["The Accelerators at a glance", showing the five components of the RVC that are explained in the following text.]

Let’s break down what these accelerators do:

The Creator: An assistant that simplifies project setup, drastically reducing the administrative overhead of starting a new requirements evaluation process.
The Decomposer: This is brilliant. It automatically breaks down complex requirements and identifies critical information such as priority and the relevant subject-matter experts.
The Collaboration Handler: Seamlessly shares requirements with the right people—internal and external—handling the technical prerequisites for standardized collaborations so your teams can focus on engineering, not IT setup.
The Cockpit: A central dashboard that provides a real-time overview of your projects. It tracks deadlines, completion status, and fulfillment degrees, ensuring you always have a clear picture of where things stand.
The Explorer: A smart search function that helps you find similar, previously implemented requirements and their solutions. This is a huge time-saver and promotes knowledge reuse across the organization

Together, these a accelerators transform requirements engineering from a reactive, manual process into a proactive, automated, and data-driven function.

From the tool to real-life collaboration - Real Benefits in Action

Now how can design and product teams leverage SAP Integrated Product Development to develop digital product structures, formulations and recipes in a real collaboration and across system boundaries?

["Overview of Specification Management in SAP IPD, including attributes and composition and collaboration scenarios.]


The SAP IPD supports real collaboration as it resolves these real-life challenges:

1. No More Confusion: Everyone, from R&D to procurement, uses the same updated information, reducing mistakes and improving product quality. The RVC provides a clear, real-time dashboard that displays the degree of completion and fulfillment for every requirement in your project. This delivers complete transparency and control over the entire requirements evaluation process. Project managers can instantly identify potential bottlenecks, track progress against deadlines, and provide accurate status updates to stakeholders.

2. Easy Supplier Collaboration: SAP IPD lets you share precise data with suppliers, avoiding tedious manual data entry and making sure that nothing falls through the cracks.

3. Stay Ahead of Compliance: With detailed product breakdowns, you'd easily track and ensure products meet regulations like REACH. If something is flagged, you know exactly where it's used so you can fix it quickly.

["Overview of platform-based value creation, including OEM, Mechanic, production engineer, and other roles and their functions.]

If you manage your product or material specifications centrally and consistently—and share them seamlessly with your suppliers—SAP IPD Specification Management together with CENIT provides the ideal platform and partner to ensure your success.


Seamlessly Integrated into Your SAP Landscape

Now, for the part I find most compelling: this isn't a standalone tool that creates yet another data silo.
The CENIT Requirements Valuation Cockpit is designed to be optimally integrated with your existing SAP IPD environment.

["Integration diagram, outlining how the CENIT RVC plugs into the SAP ecosystem, working alongside SAP IPD Requirements Management and SAP S/4HANA.]

As you can see, the RVC complements and enhances the standard capabilities of SAP IPD for Requirements Management and SAP IPD for Collaboration. Whether you are in a Request for Quotation (RFx) process with a customer or deep within a formal Product Engineering Process (PEP), the data flows seamlessly. This means you can manage, evaluate, and prioritize requirements using the specialized tool RVC while ensuring that all decisions and resulting data are anchored in your single source of truth: SAP S/4HANA. This integration ensures data continuity from the earliest customer wish all the way through to industrialization, embodying the end-to-end vision of the digital thread.

Conclusion: The Power of the Ecosystem

Achieving a truly "Fast, Smart, Digital" product development lifecycle requires a robust digital core and a vibrant partner ecosystem. CENIT's Requirements Valuation Cockpit is a prime example of how a partner solution can provide targeted, high-impact functionality that accelerates a specific part of the product lifecycle, all while integrating smoothly into the broader SAP landscape.

By automating collaboration, enabling intelligent assessment, and providing clear visibility, this platform helps turn the complexities of requirements engineering into a competitive advantage.

To learn more about enhancing your product development processes, I encourage you to explore the SAP Community for discussions on SAP Integrated Product Development and keep an eye out for partner sessions from experts like CENIT.

If you want to see a demo of the RVC and learn more, register to access the recordings of our two Meet the expert webinars via the SAP Supply Chain Management Enablement Hub.

More Information / Additional Links 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 
Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

entry.title: Re: How to Configure Replenishment Jobs (SAP Commerce B2B)
entry.content:
Go to Backoffice and search for replenishment jobs under the B2B order section. Set your trigger, either date-based or threshold-based depending on your use case. Most B2B setups use threshold since it fires automatically when stock drops. Make sure the job is linked to the correct base store and that the replenishment template has the right unit and quantity values. Cronjob needs to be active and scheduled, a lot of people configure everything correctly but forget to activate the trigger.

What is your specific setup, date based or threshold based replenishment?

entry.title: 🇿🇦 SAP Stammtisch Cape Town is Back – Join Us in Hout Bay!
entry.content:
After a great start last year, the SAP Stammtisch Cape Town is back for its first gathering in 2026—and we’re kicking things off in a very special location.

📍 Kronendal 1713 Restaurant, Hout Bay
📅 26th of March, 2026
🕕 From 18:30 onwards

A New Year, A Fresh Start

The SAP community in Cape Town continues to grow—and so does the need for a space where we can meet, exchange ideas, and discuss what’s really happening in our ecosystem.

This Time: Insights from Connecting SAP Fusion

Our first meetup of the year will focus on one key topic:

👉 What did we learn from Connecting SAP Fusion in Johannesburg?

The recent event in Sandton brought together SAP experts, partners, and customers from across South Africa. One theme clearly stood out:

AI is reshaping how SAP solutions are built and used.

We’ll bring those insights to Cape Town and discuss:

  • AI in SAP – hype vs. real use cases
  • What it means for developers and architects
  • How SAP BTP and modern extensions are evolving
  • What customers in South Africa should prepare for

Why You Should Join

Whether you are:

  • Developer
  • Architect
  • Functional consultant
  • Freelancer
  • Or just curious about SAP

👉 This is your space to connect.

Expect:

  • Honest discussions
  • Different perspectives
  • New connections

And most importantly—a strong local SAP community

A Unique Venue

This time we meet at Kronendal 1713 Restaurant in Hout Bay—a place full of history and character.

A perfect setting for good conversations, great food, and a relaxed evening with fellow SAP professionals.

Let’s Keep Building the Community

The SAP Stammtisch Cape Town started with a simple idea:
Bring people together.

And that’s exactly what we’ll continue to do.

entry.title: Re: It is not possible to configure the “Reason for Refus...
entry.content:
Good day @Joan

Thank you for your question.

Yes, this is already available in Public Cloud. What you seek is described in Configure Bill of Exchange Processing. So, this is not currently a missing feature.

See the following reference for more details.

Configuring Bill of Exchange Processing | SAP Help Portal

If you cannot find it in your tenant, then the issue is most likely one of these:

  1. You have not activated the relevant Bill of Exchange scope.
  2. Your role does not include the required configuration authorisations
  3. You are looking in a different configuration area than the Bill of Exchange Processing
  4. You are expecting a receivable setting while the document setting is under the payable return processing configuration.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Required Automation Of Delivery & PGI Creation and Billing Documents In AIL Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

  1. QR Code scanning
    The sales person scans the QR codes of all trolleys and enters the Sales Order Number and Vehicle Number in the system during the scanning process.
  2. Submission
    After completing the scanning of all trolleys, the sales person clicks the “Submit” button.
  3. Reference Number Generation
    Once the Submit button is clicked, the system generates a Reference Number.
    Under this reference number, the following documents are automatically generated:
  • Invoice
  • E-Invoice
  • E-Way Bill
  • Digital Signature
  1. Bill Download
    The sales person opens the application on the computer and enters the Reference Number and Vehicle Number.
    The Download button will appear. After clicking it, all bills related to that vehicle will be downloaded.
  2. Trip Sheet Generation
    After entering the Reference Number in the Trip Sheet, all invoices related to that reference will appear in the table.
    Finally, click the Save button to generate the Trip Sheet.
  3. Is the Automatic Delivery and PGI creation in SAP Public Cloud?

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Re: Launch default app in Build Workzone site
entry.content:
Posting a workaround as I couldn't find any such options and I noticed this question after a long time.

The requirement was to redirect the user to a particular app when the site loads. (in this case loading a maintenance page when the application is under maintenance)
I have updates the init() function in component.js of one the plugin in the application with a logic that checks the flag for the maintenance (either from backend of any other source)
Then if that flag is valid, simply redirect the page using the Hash of maintenance page

this._navigateToHash("#yourpage-display");

 Then loaded this plugin as part of the shell, which helps to inject this code in every app (helps when the user directly navigate to a particular URL).

Note: There could be a one or two second delay before redirecting as the application loading the plugin. Not recommended for sensitive contents.

entry.title: Architecture Decisions for Tax Management
entry.content:
Summary

This article provides guidance on defining a target application architecture for Global Tax Management capabilities in an SAP-centric landscape.

It is intended as a decision accelerator for enterprise and solution architects, governance bodies, and global process owners who need to balance regulatory compliance, operational efficiency, and total cost of ownership when choosing between embedded SAP S/4HANA capabilities, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC, cloud edition), and SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (PaPM Cloud).

Business Requirements

An overview of the business capabilities includes:



Typical business requirements in Tax Management include:

i. Tax Determination and Calculation

  • Ability to identify tax relevance in business transactions and activities (involving VAT, use tax, sales tax, and the like). It includes calculating taxes for the relevant business transactions of involved entities, based on applicable tax-calculation parameters such as tax codes and jurisdiction codes.

ii. Tax Operations

  • Ability to manage transactional tax assets, liabilities and payments in accordance with applicable tax law and regulations.

iii. Tax Settlement

  • Ability to apply a period-end process for corporate taxes based on data from tax journal processing. It includes application of tax-specific valuation rules, such as depreciation of assets or revaluation of expenses, to comply with local tax regulations.

iv. Tax Classification

  • Ability to tag business transactions for corporate taxes.

v. Document and Reporting Compliance

  • Ability to generate declarations and documents to be submitted in real-time or periodically, in a predetermined physical or electronic format, for legal, statutory and regulatory compliance.

vi. Tax Control Management

  • Ability to manage a company-wide control system with a repository of compliance checks to automatically detect and correct inaccurate tax postings.

vii. Excise Tax Management

  • Ability to track, document, and report all relevant movements of excisable goods, based on either processes or company-specific or legal requirements.

Solution Options

The International Trade & Tax Management area is characterized by having multiple solutions that together cover a wide spectrum of business requirements. To look at solution options, we first split the analysis between Tax Management and International Trade Management.

For Tax Management, the solution options in the portfolio are:

  1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  2. SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition
  3. SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud
  4. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (cloud edition).

Characterization of the Solution Options

The solution options are considered within the single domain of Tax Management. Global tax requirements are typically addressed by combining several of these products rather than relying on a single solution.


1. SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Role in the architecture
Core cloud ERP providing:

  • tax determination and calculation in operational processes (Order-to-Cash, Procure-to-Pay, etc.)
  • tax classification of master data and transactions
  • integration to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (cloud edition) for digital reporting (depending on the country in scope DRC Cloud may not
  • foundational data for tax operations, tax settlement and tax control management

Typical use cases

  • Greenfield cloud ERP implementations
  • Standardized tax processing in organizations aiming to simplify and harmonize processes globally
  • Landscapes where tax rules can largely be handled via standard S/4HANA configuration plus DRC

 

2. SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition

Role in the architecture
Core ERP platform for customers requiring:

  • extended flexibility in tax configuration and integration
  • support for complex legacy scenarios and coexistence with on-premise add-ons
  • a direct path from ECC while retaining a high degree of control

Typical use cases

  • Brownfield or selective data transition scenarios from ECC
  • Countries or business units with more complex indirect tax and corporate tax processes
  • Environments where integration to specialized tax add-ons or custom logic is required in addition to DRC


3. SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud

 

 

Role in the architecture
Scenario-based calculation and simulation platform used to:

  • model and simulate tax impacts (e.g. effective tax rate, transfer pricing effects, country-specific tax positions)
  • perform allocations, multi-step calculations and what-if analysis based on financial and operational data
  • support income tax planning, forecasting and control activities on top of transactional data from S/4HANA or other sources

Typical use cases

  • Groups with advanced tax planning, BEPS and transfer pricing requirements
  • Finance and tax functions that need transparent, traceable models for tax settlement and tax control.
  • Income tax calculation

For more details visit SAP Profitability and Performance Management.

 

4. SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (cloud edition)

 


Role in the architecture
Central solution for digital tax and regulatory reporting:

  • generation and submission of e-invoices and other e-documents (inbound and outbound)
  • support for continuous transaction controls (CTC) and real-time/near real-time reporting
  • periodic statutory and tax reporting based on transactional data from S/4HANA and other systems
  • monitoring cockpit for document status, rejections and corrections

Typical use cases

  • Countries with e-invoicing and CTC mandates
  • Standardization of digital reporting processes across multiple entities and jurisdictions
  • Use as a central hub for digital reporting across several SAP backends

For additional details, please visit SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Criteria to Determine the Most Suitable Target Solution

When choosing the right combination of Tax Management solutions, it is helpful to look at the decision criteria from a feature and capability perspective. The questions below can be used to assess which product (or combination) is most appropriate.

1. Core Transactional Tax Capabilities

  • Do you require standardized tax determination and calculation across Order-to-Cash and Procure-to-Pay, based on configurable tax codes, condition techniques and jurisdiction structures?
  • Are there requirements for multiple indirect tax types (VAT/GST, sales & use tax, withholding tax, excise) to be handled consistently in one transactional backbone?
  • Is there a need for central tax logic and configuration across several company codes or countries, or can local configurations remain independent?

These criteria help determine whether SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition is sufficient as the main transactional tax engine in most cases (with the exception of Tax Jurisdiction based calculation cases such as USA or Canada, where the SAP guidance is to use External Tax Calculation Capability such as Vertex or Thomson Reuters ONESOURCE), and how far standard configuration can be leveraged.

2. Digital Tax & Reporting Capabilities

  • Do you operate in countries that require e-invoicing, CTC (Continuous Transaction Controls), real-time reporting, or standardized electronic file formats (for example, SAF-T)?
  • How many different document types and submission channels (e.g. invoices, credit notes, transport documents, periodic returns) need to be supported?
  • Is there a requirement to monitor submission status, manage rejections, and handle resubmissions from a central cockpit?
  • Do you need a single digital reporting layer that can serve multiple S/4HANA or ECC systems (and possibly non-SAP systems), or will each ERP handle its own submissions?

These criteria drive the scope and deployment model for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (cloud edition).

3. Tax Planning & Simulation Capabilities

  • Do you need to perform what-if analysis and simulations on effective tax rate, group tax position, transfer pricing scenarios, or BEPS-related effects?
  • Is there a requirement to support multi-step allocation and calculation logic that goes beyond what is practical in the ERP (for example, complex allocation keys, layered calculations, iterative models)?
  • Do tax and finance stakeholders require traceable calculation logic (with clear lineage from inputs to outputs) for audit and documentation purposes?
  • Are you planning to align tax planning, forecasting and budgeting tightly with the actuals in SAP S/4HANA?

These criteria indicate whether SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (PaPM Cloud) or its S/4HANA Cloud, private edition deployment variant should be part of the target architecture.

4. Control, Monitoring & Audit Capabilities

  • How important is it to have central visibility of tax-relevant controls, checks and exception handling across entities and countries?
  • Do you require detailed audit trails on how tax amounts were derived, including calculation steps and data sources?
  • Is there a need for automated detection of inconsistencies (for example, mismatches between transactional data and submitted returns) and structured remediation workflows?

These criteria help to determine the relative emphasis on PaPM Cloud (for analytical control and simulation) and DRC (for operational monitoring of digital reporting).

5. Integration & Extensibility Features

  • Do you need to bring tax-relevant data from multiple SAP and non-SAP systems into a single place for reporting and analysis?
  • Are there existing or planned external tax engines or local compliance solutions that need to be integrated into the architecture?
  • Is there a need for reusable APIs and integration patterns for tax documents and reporting across several landscapes?

These criteria influence how DRC and PaPM Cloud are positioned as integration and aggregation layers on top of S/4HANA.

6. Deployment & Operational Features

  • Is your target deployment model public cloud, private cloud, or a mix of both, and how tightly do you want to couple tax functionality to the ERP release cycle?
  • What are your expectations around change agility (for example, how quickly new country mandates or tax rules must be implemented)?
  • What level of operational complexity (monitoring, support, upgrades) is acceptable for tax-related solutions?

These criteria help decide between S/4HANA Cloud vs. S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, and inform whether PaPM Cloud and DRC (cloud edition) are operated centrally or per system.

 

Evaluation Spreadsheet

The evaluation spreadsheet offers a structured way to compare the Tax Management solution options against the criteria above.

 

Decision Tree

The decision tree is intended to guide architects and business stakeholders through a small number of key questions and arrive at a short list of suitable Tax Management solution patterns.

 



Conclusion

For most organizations, SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition forms the transactional backbone for end-to-end tax determination, classification and operations.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (cloud edition) extends this core by addressing the growing number of global digital tax and reporting mandates, providing a unified way to handle e-invoicing, CTC and periodic statutory submissions.

For organizations requiring deeper tax planning, simulation and control analytics, SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (and its deployment variant for S/4HANA Cloud, private edition) adds a powerful, scenario-based modelling layer on top of the transactional backbone.

Together, these solutions enable a robust and flexible Global Tax Management architecture:

  • S/4HANA Cloud / private edition for core tax processes,
  • DRC (cloud edition) for digital compliance and statutory reporting, and
  • PaPM Cloud for strategic tax insight and scenario analysis.

This combination allows organizations to standardize tax operations, reduce compliance risk, and build a scalable foundation for ongoing digital tax transformation in the SAP ecosystem.

entry.title: 5 Things You Need to Know Before Starting with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA is one of the most in-demand skills in today’s SAP ecosystem, but it can feel overwhelming to understand what it actually is and why it matters. 

Is it just a database upgrade? A completely new system? And why are so many companies rushing to adopt it before 2027? 

In this guide, alongside João Simões , Principal Software Architect at SAP Portugal, we break down SAP S/4HANA in simple terms: what it is, how it differs from SAP ECC, why companies are migrating, and the key things you need to know before getting started. 

What is SAP S/4HANA? 

 is a modern Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) suite designed to help organizations run their core business processes in real time. It is built on the SAP HANA (in-memory database), which allows companies to process and analyze large volumes of data instantly. As a result, this ERP delivers significantly faster performance than traditional systems. 

Since it is designed to centralize business processes, SAP S/4HANA integrates major business functions such as finance, supply chain, manufacturing, HR, and sales into a single platform, providing users with a more intuitive and mobile-friendly experience through Versions Available 

  • Cloud (S/4HANA Cloud): SaaS offering with regular updates
  • On-Premise (S/4HANA): Deployed on company servers with more customization options 

Why Are Companies Moving to SAP S/4HANA? 

Transitioning to SAP S/4HANA has become critical because January 1st 2027, marks the end of mainstream support for SAP ERP (Business Suite). After this date, organizations running SAP ECC will no longer receive new security patches, bug fixes, compliance & audit risks, or technical support from SAP.  

Continuing to operate with unsupported systems can expose companies to increasing cybersecurity risks, compliance risks, and higher operational costs. For many organizations, this creates a limited migration window of the next 2-3 years to plan and execute their transition to SAP S/4HANA.  

However, migrating to S/4HANA is not only driven by the deadline, but also by 3 key factors:

  • Financial risks → Running unsupported systems can lead to cybersecurity vulnerabilities, regulatory risks (eg. GDPR), and higher costs for maintaining legacy infrastructure. 
  • Technical obsolescence → Legacy ERP systems can’t integrate with Industry 4.0 tech such as #cloud, #AI, and #IoT, making business processes increasingly brittle, without mentioning that staff expertise of old systems becomes unavailable.  
  • Competitive disadvantage → Modern businesses require real-time insights, agility, Cloud flexibility, and a digital-first UX, capabilities that SAP S/4HANA is designed to support. 

SAP ECC vs SAP S/4HANA: Key Differences Explained 

Feature 

SAP ECC 

SAP S/4HANA 

Database 

Oracle, SQL Server, DB2 

SAP HANA only 

Architecture 

Three-tier (Presentation, Application, Database) 

Simplified, HANA-optimized 

In-Memory Processing 

Limited 

Built-in & native 

Performance 

Traditional disk-based; slower 

Real-time processing; 10x faster 

Reporting & Analytics 

Limited analytics capabilities 

Instant analytics & reporting 

Data Model 

Complex structures; many aggregation tables 

Simplified tables; reduced redundancy 

Storage Requirements 

High 

Reduced 

User Interface 

Older, traditional interface 

Modern Fiori interface 

Mobility 

Limited mobile support 

Mobile-friendly 

AI/ML Capabilities 

Not available 

Integrated AI/ML 

IoT Integration 

Limited 

Full IoT support 

Cloud Ready 

Not optimized for cloud 

Cloud-native 

Upfront Cost 

Lower 

Higher 

Operational Cost 

High maintenance overhead 

Lower operational costs 

Maintenance 

Complex, time-consuming 

Reduced maintenance 

End of Support 

2027 (mainstream support) 

Actively supported & developed 

User Experience 

Functional but dated 

Intuitive & modern 

What does SAP S/4HANA actually does? (Core Capabilities) 

As mentioned before, it enables organizations make faster decisions and operate more efficiently.  Core capabilities include: 

  1. Real-Time Analytics & Reporting
  2. AI & Machine Learning
    • Demand Forecasting: AI predicts product demand to optimize inventory
    • Fraud Detection: Machine learning flags suspicious transactions automatically
    • Equipment Maintenance: Predictive models warn of failures before they happen
    • Customer Analytics: AI identifies high-value customers and churn risks
  3. User Experience via SAP Fiori
  4. Business Process Automation
  5. Simplified Data Model & Performance
    • Faster reporting: Reports that took 30 minutes now take 2 seconds
    • Lower costs: Reduced storage and server infrastructure
    • Better decisions: Real-time data vs. stale data
    • Easier maintenance: Fewer tables to manage
  6. Financial Management & Planning
  7. Supply Chain Optimization
    • Reduced stockouts: 15–20% inventory reduction
    • Faster delivery: Optimized routes & automated processes
    • Cost savings: 10–15% logistics cost reduction
    • Risk mitigation: Early warning of supply disruptions
  8. Sales & Customer Experience
  9. Integration with External Systems
  10. Compliance & Risk Management
  11. Cloud & Scalability
  12. Enhanced Planning & Forecasting

To illustrate the impact, consider a bakery that supplies pastries to cafés and supermarkets. The order-to-cash process can change significantly when moving from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA:  

Phase 

Before (ECC) 

After (S/4HANA) 

Order Received 

Manual entry, 2 hours 

Auto-captured, instant 

Inventory Check 

Query & wait, 30 mins 

Real-time visibility, 1 second 

Pricing 

Manual calculation, errors 

Dynamic AI pricing, instant 

Quote 

Created next day 

Sent in 5 minutes 

Production Plan 

Scheduled weekly 

Optimized hourly by AI 

Shipment 

Routed manually 

AI optimizes for cost/speed 

Invoice 

Generated, sent week later 

Auto-generated at shipment 

Payment 

30-day terms average 

Accelerated to 15 days 

How To Pick The Right SAP S/4HANA Deployment Model? 

Organizations can deploy SAP S/4HANA in several ways depending on their business needs, level of customization, and IT strategy. The three main deployment options are:  

Dimension  

Public Cloud (GROW) 

Private Cloud (RISE) 

On-Premise 

Innovation Cycle 

Semi-annual 

FPS 6 months; Major releases 2 years 

 

TCO 

Lowest 

Lower 

Higher 

License 

Subscription 

Subscription 

Software License 

IT Infra 

SAP, shared 

Dedicated 

Customer Managed 

Customizations 

Within Standards 

Within Standards 

Open-to-mods 

Governance 

SAP-led 

Customer-influenced 

Customer-led 

How Much Does SAP S/4HANA Cost? 

SAP S/4HANA pricing can vary significantly depending on the organization’s size, system complexity, and deployment model. Instead of a fixed price, the total cost of ownership (TCO) depends on several factors, such as:  

  • Number of users (biggest factor) 
  • Deployment type (cloud vs on-premise) 
  • Modules required (Finance, Supply Chain, HR, etc.) 
  • Industry-specific solutions 
  • Implementation complexity
  • Company size & revenue  

Cloud vs On-Premise Comparison 

Aspect 

Cloud 

On-Premise 

Initial licensing cost 

Lower 

Higher 

Infrastructure cost 

Included 

Higher 

Implementation time 

Faster 

Slower 

Total Cost of Ownership (3-5 years) 

Often lower 

Often higher 

Is SAP S/4HANA Difficult To Learn? 

No. Like any enterprise system, SAP S/4HANA comes with a learning curve but is more than doable! Depending on the learning track, you can get certified between 2 weeks up to 2 months.  

For those interested in building their skills in this area, the SAP Young Professionals program offers a fully sponsored, world class training experience, where you will earn SAP Certifications, gain hands-on experience with SAP solutions, and get introduced into the SAP Ecosystem to work with Partners, Clients, and even SAP. Check out our current openings.  

If none of the openings are suitable for your region at the moment, we invite you to explore all courses on SAP Learning, where you can get certifications with self-paced e-learning courses.
